metadata.title: States PCS: Madhya Pradesh GK Questions For MPPSC Examinations

questions.question: In which year did the Central Government propose the Navagam Dam on Narmada River?
questions.explanation: The Navagam Dam proposal was made in 1963 by the Central Government to build a 425-foot dam on the Narmada River in Gujarat. This plan faced opposition from Madhya Pradesh due to concerns over uneven power distribution. The Narmada River is a major west-flowing river in India, and multiple dams have been constructed for irrigation and hydroelectric projects.

questions.question: Which is the largest river valley project in Madhya Pradesh?
questions.explanation: The Narmada Valley Project is the largest river valley project in Madhya Pradesh, incorporating major hydroelectric and irrigation projects like Indira Sagar and Omkareshwar. Other projects like Tawa, Gandhi Sagar, and Jawahar Sagar are significant but smaller in scale compared to the expansive Narmada system.

questions.question: Eran archaeological site lies on the banks of which river?
questions.explanation: Eran is located on the banks of the Betwa River near Bina tehsil, Sagar district, Madhya Pradesh. It dates from the Gupta period and contains inscriptions and archaeological remains. The Betwa river valley was an early cultural settlement area. Eran is significant for historic and archaeological research in Madhya Pradesh.

questions.question: Hinglajgarh archaeological site is in which Madhya Pradesh district?
questions.explanation: Hinglajgarh is located in Bhanpura tehsil of Mandsaur district, Madhya Pradesh. It is a state-protected monument with fort remains and stone sculptures. The site dates from the Gupta period to the 14th century. It lies within a forest sanctuary area in Mandsaur district. Hinglajgarh has yielded sculptures of Vinayaki, Kaumari, Bhuvaneshwari, and Baglamukhi. These deities belong to the Shakti tradition. The site is associated with Tantric and Shakta worship. It is recognized as a Shakti Peetha.

questions.question: On which riverbank is Madhya Pradesh’s Simhastha (Kumbh) Fair held?
questions.explanation: The Simhastha Kumbh fair occurs every 12 years on the Shipra River in Ujjain, Madhya Pradesh. Ujjain is one of the four cities where Kumbh Melas are held, along with Prayagraj, Haridwar, and Nashik.

questions.question: In Madhya Pradesh, gum is classified as which type of forest produce?
questions.explanation: Gum is a non-timber forest produce in Madhya Pradesh, harvested without felling trees. Such produce, including tendu leaves, lac, and harra, supports forest-dependent communities and promotes sustainable forest use by minimizing timber extraction.

questions.question: In which year was the Panna Biosphere Reserve established?
questions.explanation: The Panna Biosphere Reserve was designated by the Indian government in 2011. Panna National Park was established in 1981. It became a Project Tiger Reserve in 1994. In 2020, it was included in UNESCO’s Man and the Biosphere Programme.

questions.question: What was Madhya Pradesh’s total population in the 2011 Census?
questions.explanation: The 2011 Census recorded Madhya Pradesh’s population as 72,626,809, including 37.6 million males and 35 million females. It is the second-most populous state in central India after Uttar Pradesh. This data guides governmental planning in health, education, and infrastructure development within the state.

questions.question: Which villages in Rewa district are the original homeland of the Kol tribe?
questions.explanation: The Kol tribe of Madhya Pradesh traces its origin to Farenda and Kurali villages in Rewa district. These villages are culturally significant as the ancestral homeland, from where the Kol people dispersed to other areas in the region, reflecting their historical settlement patterns within Madhya Pradesh.

questions.question: In which regions of Madhya Pradesh is the Saharia tribe mainly found?
questions.explanation: The term ‘Saharia’ means “one who lives with lions.” Saharia tribe primarily inhabits the north-western districts of Madhya Pradesh, especially in Gwalior and Chambal divisions. They are one of the most prominent tribal communities here, with smaller populations scattered in other divisions. These regions form a significant part of their cultural and social identity within the state.
